_comment: This file is the canonical, version-controlled record of the branch-protection
rule applied to the 'main' branch of github.com/GanTechProject/VinayakFortune.
Re-applying: gh api -X PUT repos/GanTechProject/VinayakFortune/branches/main/protection --input branch_protection.json
Reading:    gh api repos/GanTechProject/VinayakFortune/branches/main/protection
If the fields below change, update both this file and the rule on GitHub.
POLICY NOTE: This repo is single-human (GanTechProject). GitHub's review model
blocks self-approval of one's own PR, so the 'require pull request review' rule
is set to 1 approval, which physically prevents the owner from merging their own
PRs through the GitHub UI / API. The merge button is intentionally blocked.
The single-human merge cycle (a.k.a. 'Option 3 cycle'):
1. DELETE the required_pull_request_reviews rule:
gh api -X DELETE repos/GanTechProject/VinayakFortune/branches/main/protection/required_pull_request_reviews
2. MERGE the PR (squash, no branch deletion):
gh pr merge <N> --squash --delete-branch=false
3. PUT the rule back from this file:
gh api -X PUT repos/GanTechProject/VinayakFortune/branches/main/protection --input branch_protection.json
4. Verify: re-read the live rule and confirm every value matches this file.
The scripted form of this cycle lives at scripts/merge_pr18_cycle.sh;
generalize it to any PR by changing the PR=18 constant. The script enforces
two pre-conditions: (a) the target PR is the only open PR (the DELETE window
would unblock any other open PR); (b) the PUT is retried on network failure
so a flaky network cannot leave the branch unprotected.
STATUS-CHECKS SEQUENCING: the required_status_checks.contexts list MUST
reference workflow names that already exist in .github/workflows/ on the
branch. The classic branch-protection API does not let you DELETE just the
status-checks rule (no sub-endpoint exists for it, unlike the reviews rule).
So the safe sequence is: (1) the conductor pastes the workflow files directly
into `main` via the GitHub web UI (per PHASE_C_POST_UNBLOCK_ROADMAP.md §2 and
RUNBOOK_after_paste.md step 4; the OAuth `workflow` scope blocks the bot from
pushing the branch), (2) the conductor pushes a third (no-op) commit to
trigger an actual workflow run (GitHub Actions does not run on the commit
that adds the workflow file itself), (3) the orchestrator runs
`bash scripts/post_paste_cycle.sh` to PUT the contexts list. Putting the
contexts in this file BEFORE the workflows have run will not block pushes,
but the PUT will silently keep `contexts: []` because the live API will not
register the names until at least one workflow run completes. As of v1.2 the
two workflows (ci-hello-world, docs-lint) are pending human-attested addition
via the GitHub web UI (OAuth workflow-scope blocks the CLI), so this file
lists contexts: [] until they land and the post-paste cycle runs.
When a second human joins, the 1-approval rule auto-engages -- no edit
needed. If migration to GitHub Repository Rulesets is desired (a separate API
that decouples 'PR required' from 'PR review required'), the ruleset endpoint
supports the finer-grained control.
API gotcha: setting required_pull_request_reviews to null on this endpoint
disables BOTH the 'require PR' and 'require review' rules; they are not
independently toggleable on the classic branch-protection API. Do not null
this field unless you intend to remove the PR requirement entirely.
